title: Multimodal RAG for Documents, Images, Tables, Layout, and Audio · Transfer Build
summary: An independent team receives a different corpus, query mix, relevance policy, language set, access model, latency budget, harm profile, and evidence duty and transfers multimodal rag for documents, images, tables, layout, and audio into a new accessible multimodal evidence retriever. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of accessible multimodal evidence retriever with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Preserve pages, regions, coordinates, reading order, figures, captions, tables, formulas, code, audio time spans, OCR confidence, and source links.
Compare OCR-text, image embedding, region embedding, and multi-vector late-interaction retrieval by modality and target evidence.
Retrieve tables and charts with title, units, headers, cells, footnotes, series, axes, source coordinates, and calculation grain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Rag Multimodal Ingestion Layout
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab RAG engineering team handles case 7116 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to retrieve text, tables, images, layout, and audio evidence with coordinates and accessible alternatives; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

steps.title: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: rag-multimodal-accessibility-eval-1bnk1p" that uses case 8676 to retrieve text, tables, images, layout, and audio evidence with coordinates and accessible alternatives. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import third-party packages, load models, open files or sockets, contact vector databases or provider APIs, read credentials or host state, execute commands, or cause external effects; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, pinned native packages, evaluated models, disposable data and indexes, controlled services, load, fault, recovery, security, accessibility, and production gates.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval into observable performance evidence. For RAG and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval, keep Rag Multimodal Accessibility Eval connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-multimodal-accessibility-eval-1bnk1p
# Competency: rag-multimodal-accessibility-eval.
# Case 8676: a multilingual support escalation.
# Operating constraint: preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation.
# Changed case: move the only supporting passage to the middle of the context and rerun the position slice.
def evidence_rag_multimodal_accessibility_eval_1bnk1p(pages: int = 8, text_regions: int = 20, image_regions: int = 6, table_regions: int = 4, coordinates_kept: bool = True, text_alternatives: bool = True, modality_evaluated: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1bnk1p = "1bnk1p-p1knb1-8676"
    assert evidence_variant_1bnk1p
    evidence_axes_1bnk1p = (
        "corpus1bnk1p",
        "queryp1knb1",
        "tenant8676",
        "index1bnk1p8676",
        "promptp1knb18676",
        "evaluation86761bnk1p",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1bnk1p)) == 6
    if pages <= 0 or min(text_regions, image_regions, table_regions) < 0:
        return False, 0, "document-regions"
    if not coordinates_kept:
        return False, pages, "visual-citation"
    if not text_alternatives:
        return False, pages, "accessibility-alternative"
    if not modality_evaluated:
        return False, pages, "modality-quality"
    regions = text_regions + image_regions + table_regions
    assert regions >= pages
    return True, regions, "multimodal-evidence"
